expectations.text: DashboardLayoutмқҙ folder кё°л°ҳ index.tsx нҢЁн„ҙмңјлЎң м •лҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: The output is a single DashboardLayout.tsx file with inline sub-components (Sidebar, DashboardHeader, MobileNav defined as private functions). No folder-based index.tsx pattern was created. The transcript confirms: 'Sub-components extracted inline вҖ” Sidebar, DashboardHeader, MobileNav as private functions within the file, keeping the file self-contained.'

The output file is still components/DashboardLayout.tsx. The transcript states the file was refactored in place: 'Refactored the monolithic component following project conventions' but the output path remains outputs/DashboardLayout.tsx, and the transcript does not mention creating a folder or removing the flat file.
A dedicated Sidebar sub-component exists at line 80 with <aside> element, data-testid='dashboard-sidebar', containing logo, navigation links, and footer sections.
A dedicated DashboardHeader sub-component exists at line 149 with <header> element, data-testid='dashboard-header', containing title, mobile logo, and right slot.
A <main> element exists at line 258 with data-testid='dashboard-main', wrapping {children} with appropriate padding and overflow classes.

expectations.text: children мӨ‘мӢ¬ shell м—ӯн• мқҙ мң м§ҖлҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: DashboardLayoutProps includes 'children: ReactNode' (line 7). The main component accepts children and renders them inside <main> at line 262: '{children}'. The component acts as a shell composing Sidebar, DashboardHeader, main content, and MobileNav.

expectations.text: responsive breakpoint classк°Җ мң м§Җлҗҳкұ°лӮҳ к°ңм„ лҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: Multiple responsive breakpoint classes present: md:flex, md:w-60, lg:w-64, md:hidden, md:block, md:px-6, lg:px-8, md:pb-6. The transcript notes Tailwind class ordering was improved to follow mobile-first convention.

expectations.text: мӮ¬мҡ©мІҳ importк°Җ мғҲ кІҪлЎңм—җ л§һкІҢ м •лҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: The file was not moved to a new path (no folder-based restructuring occurred), so there is no new path to update imports to. The transcript mentions reading app/dashboard/page.tsx as a consumer but does not show any import path changes. No updated consumer file is in the outputs.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation нҳём¶ңмқҙ м—ҶлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: Searched the full output DashboardLayout.tsx (270 lines). None of useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation appear anywhere in the file. Only imports are ReactNode from 'react' and Link from 'next/link'.

No folder was created. The output remains a flat DashboardLayout.tsx file. The transcript notes the project already has a reference pattern at components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/ but the executor chose to refactor in-place rather than move to that folder structure.

The output file is components/DashboardLayout.tsx, not under components/dashboard/. The transcript confirms reading the existing components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/ reference pattern but did not move the file there.

claims.claim: The original DashboardLayout.tsx was a monolithic 242-line component
claims.evidence: Cannot verify вҖ” the original file is not included in outputs. The transcript states this but the source file is not available for verification.

claims.claim: Sub-components extracted inline as private functions
claims.evidence: Output file contains Sidebar (line 80), DashboardHeader (line 149), and MobileNav (line 215) as non-exported functions within the same file.
No TypeScript check output is included in the outputs directory. Must trust the transcript claim.
No ESLint output is included in the outputs directory. Must trust the transcript claim.

claims.claim: Tailwind class ordering fixed to follow mobile-first convention
claims.evidence: Output shows mobile-first patterns like 'hidden flex-col ... md:flex md:w-60 lg:w-64' (line 83) where base styles come before breakpoint overrides.

claims.claim: Props interface exported separately, component as default export
claims.evidence: Line 6: 'export interface DashboardLayoutProps' and line 245: 'export default function DashboardLayout'

suggestions.reason: This passes even though the component wasn't restructured as required вҖ” it merely checks that a sidebar exists, which was already true in the original monolithic file. Consider combining structural assertions (folder-based, domain-placement) with content assertions to avoid partial credit for incomplete refactors.

Five of eleven assertions test the file/folder restructuring, which all failed together as a block because the executor chose in-place refactoring over folder-based splitting. Consider a single higher-level assertion like 'component is split into separate files under components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/' that covers folder-structure, flat-file-removed, folder-camelCase, domain-placement, and import-updated in one discriminating check.

suggestions.assertion: мӮ¬мҡ©мІҳ importк°Җ мғҲ кІҪлЎңм—җ л§һкІҢ м •лҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ
suggestions.reason: This assertion is effectively untestable from the output alone вҖ” even if the file had been moved, the consumer page.tsx is not in the outputs directory. Consider requiring the updated consumer file in outputs.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions split into two clean clusters: structural/organizational (5 assertions, all failed) and functional/content (6 assertions, all passed). The structural cluster is redundant вҖ” they all test facets of the same decision (move to folder structure). The functional cluster passes trivially because the original file already had sidebar, header, and main content. Consider adding assertions that test the actual refactoring quality: sub-component isolation, prop interface design, or removal of duplication.
